name: Cefepime
executiveSummary: Fourth-generation cephalosporin. Effective against gram-negative bacilli (including non-fermenters: P. aeruginosa), gram-positive cocci (S. pneumoniae, methicillin-resistant S. aureus), and gram-negative cocci (N. meningitidis).
indications:
## Main indications

Fourth-generation cephalosporin. Effective against gram-negative bacilli (including non-fermenters: P. aeruginosa), gram-positive cocci (S. pneumoniae, methicillin-resistant S. aureus), and gram-negative cocci (N. meningitidis).

> Adjust according to institutional protocol and medical prescription.

pediatrico.dose: Mild and moderate infections: 50 mg/kg/dose every 12 h; severe infections (endocarditis, febrile neutropenia) 50 mg/kg/dose every 8 h, cystic fibrosis: 50 mg/kg/dose every 6 h, maximum pediatric dose: 2 g/dose. Adults: 2 g every 8–12 h.

adverseEffects:
## Adverse effects

Diarrhea, nausea, vomiting, headache, rash (2%).

bibliography.citation: Hospital de Pediatría SAMIC Prof. Dr. Juan P. Garrahan. Institutional Pharmaceutical Formulary — CefEPIME* (code 1502, ATC J01DE).
